title: Adjuvant Tranexamic Acid (TXA) Versus Surgery Alone for Adult Patients With Chronic Subdural Hematoma (CSDH)

skipped_criteria: Inclusion #1: Symptomatic CSDH confirmed on CT/MRI measuring >10mm thickness — no imaging measurement or CSDH-specific diagnosis field in schema.
Inclusion #3: Scheduled for single or double burr hole evacuation — no surgical scheduling field in schema.
Inclusion #4: No contraindication for TXA — no TXA contraindication field in schema.
Exclusion #1: Mechanical heart valve — no cardiac device history field in schema.
Exclusion #2: Newly diagnosed (last 12 months) pulmonary embolism, myocardial infarction, or strong indication for antithrombotic treatment — no diagnosis history or temporal logic in schema.
Exclusion #3: Intracranial surgery within last 6 months — no surgical history or temporal logic in schema.
Exclusion #4: Woman <40 years (partially encoded as pregnancy exclusion; the age-based sex restriction for women under 40 cannot be expressed without a combined sex+age rule).
Exclusion #5: Participation in any other clinical trial — no trial enrollment field in schema.
Exclusion #6: Life expectancy of less than one year — no prognosis or life expectancy field in schema.
Exclusion #7: Modified Rankin Scale score of 4 or 5 before hematoma — no functional independence scale field in schema.
Exclusion #8: Unfit for participation for any other reason as evaluated by the including physician — subjective clinical judgment, not encodable.
Exclusion #9: Severe renal impairment (eGFR <30 ml/min or serum creatinine >150 μmol/L) — no renal function lab fields in schema.
Exclusion #10: Known hypersensitivity or allergy to TXA — no allergy/hypersensitivity field in schema.
Exclusion #11: Inability to obtain informed consent — no consent capacity field in schema.
